skill.name: github-pr-issue
skill.description: 查看/更新 GitHub Issue、PR（含评论与 diff），并按团队规范非交互创建或修改 PR；涉及 GitHub Issue/PR 的操作时使用。
skill.summary: Use GitHub CLI workflows to read, create, and update GitHub issues and pull requests.
skill.author: DCjanus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, network access, weak cryptography, and entropy concerns. Review confirms legitimate GitHub CLI and GitHub API usage, while weak cryptography and entropy alerts are false positives from model names and Markdown content. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because it retrieves authenticated GitHub data and instructs agents to create or edit issues and pull requests.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated GitHub Data Retrieval
medium_findings.description: The helper script retrieves a GitHub token through the GitHub CLI and sends authenticated requests to GitHub API endpoints. This is consistent with the skill purpose, but it can expose private pull request content, comments, diffs, and reviewer data to the agent session when users provide private repository URLs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token retrieval and authenticated GitHub API requests are directly present. The use appears intentional and limited to GitHub, so the risk is data exposure through legitimate capability rather than confirmed malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Issue and Pull Request Write Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use non-interactive GitHub CLI commands to create issues, create pull requests, and edit pull requests. These operations are legitimate for the skill, but they can modify repository state if used without explicit user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Markdown clearly directs use of `gh issue create`, `gh pr new`, and `gh pr edit`. This is intended automation, but it has repository write impact.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines define Pydantic models and output fields such as comment, review, and node containers. No hashing, encryption, signing, or cryptographic algorithm use was found at the reported locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code contains data model and assignment logic, not cryptographic APIs. The SKILL.md line is only descriptive metadata.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Are Not Hidden Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command detections in SKILL.md are documentation examples for GitHub CLI usage. They do not execute by themselves, but they should still be treated as user-approved repository operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detections are inside Markdown instructions and fenced examples. They become operational only if the agent follows them in response to a user task.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Alert Is Not Supported By Malicious Content
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md file is short Markdown with Chinese instructions, CLI examples, and front matter. No encoded payload, binary content, or prompt injection attempt was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found readable Markdown rather than obfuscated text. The entropy heuristic is likely affected by multilingual text and command syntax.

dangerous_patterns.title: Subprocess Call To GitHub CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The script executes `gh auth token` through subprocess.run to obtain the current user's GitHub token. Arguments are fixed, so command injection risk is low, but the pattern depends on local CLI credentials.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is present and uses fixed arguments. The security concern is credential use, not shell injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated Network Requests To GitHub
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses httpx to request GitHub API resources for pull requests, comments, reviews, files, commits, diffs, and rate limit data. The destination is constrained to GitHub API, but private repository data can be returned.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network request helpers and GitHub endpoint calls are explicit. URL parsing restricts user input to github.com pull request links, reducing malicious redirect risk.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent GitHub issue and pull request workflows without repeated manual CLI steps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through reading PR context and creating or updating GitHub records.
actual_capabilities: Reads GitHub pull request metadata through a bundled Python helper.
Can include PR body, diff, reviews, comments, files, commits, statistics, and rate limit data.
Uses the local GitHub CLI authentication token for GitHub API requests.
Provides non-interactive patterns for creating GitHub issues.
Provides non-interactive patterns for creating and editing pull requests.
Defines a concise pull request body format for summary, changes, tradeoffs, testing, and notes.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI to be installed and authenticated before the helper script works.
Only supports pull request URLs on github.com.
Does not provide thread structure for review comments.
Can expose private repository data to the active AI session when used on private repositories.

use_cases.title: Review PR Context
use_cases.description: Fetch a focused view of a pull request with selected comments, reviews, files, commits, or diff content.

use_cases.title: Create Structured Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Prepare and submit a pull request with a clear title, summary, key changes, tradeoffs, and testing notes.

use_cases.title: Open Concise Issues
use_cases.description: Create GitHub issues with short, readable titles and multiline descriptions through non-interactive CLI input.

prompt_templates.title: Read a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this GitHub pull request and summarize the title, body, changed files, and test notes: <PR URL>

prompt_templates.title: Review PR Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch this pull request with reviews, review comments, and issue comments. Summarize unresolved reviewer concerns: <PR URL>

prompt_templates.title: Create an Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub issue for this bug. Use a concise title and include reproduction steps, expected behavior, and impact.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the current branch, prepare a semantic PR title, write the PR body using the team format, and create a draft PR.

output_examples.input: Read a PR with files, stats, and reviews.
output_examples.output: A concise review brief with PR title, author, changed file list, additions and deletions, reviewer states, and key discussion points.

output_examples.input: Create a pull request after changes are pushed.
output_examples.output: A GitHub pull request URL after submitting a structured title and body with summary, key changes, constraints, and testing notes.

output_examples.input: Create an issue for a regression.
output_examples.output: A GitHub issue URL after submitting a short title and a clear multiline issue description.

best_practices: Confirm repository, branch, base branch, and write intent before creating or editing issues and pull requests.
Request only the PR sections needed for the task to reduce private data exposure.
Include testing evidence or clearly state why testing was not performed.
anti_patterns: Do not run write commands against a repository without explicit user approval.
Do not include private diffs, comments, or review content in public summaries.
Do not create vague PR bodies that only list implementation steps.

faq.question: Does this skill require GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The helper script calls GitHub CLI to obtain the current authentication token.

faq.question: Can it read private pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes, if the authenticated GitHub account has access. Private data can enter the AI session.

faq.question: Can it create pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides non-interactive GitHub CLI commands for creating and editing pull requests.

faq.question: Can it create GitHub issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides a non-interactive issue creation pattern using a body file.

faq.question: Does it support GitHub Enterprise Server?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The helper script only accepts github.com pull request URLs.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium warning because it uses local GitHub credentials and can modify repository state.
